channel.title: Online-Right on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Online-Right on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Podcaster Uses Terms “Grand Substitution” And “Large Swap” To Avoid Phrase That Got Him Suspended
item.description:
Doom podcaster Julian May has returned from a seven-day platform suspension with a renewed commitment to free speech, provided that speech is expressed through increasingly desperate synonyms unlikely to trigger automated moderation.

May, host of Mayday, told viewers he would no longer use “the phrase he can no longer say”, instead warning of a “grand substitution”, a “large swap” and an “extended surrogate process”.

“I am not saying the phrase,” May said. “You know the phrase. They know the phrase. The people doing the civilisation-wide population reassignment event know the phrase. But I am not saying it, because apparently the danger lies not in the event itself, but in allowing a man with the wrong opinions to speak of the event.”

item.title: Content Creator Host Focuses On Immigration-Housing Link After Realising “No Shelter” Harder To Lifestyle-Hack Than Traffic Or Medicare Collapse
item.description:
SYDNEY — A YouTube content creator has explained his continued focus on the relationship between immigration and housing, rather than immigration and every other overloaded national system, after realising that shelter remains one of the few social goods Australians cannot simply avoid needing through better habits, lower expectations, or leaving the house less.

Commentator Julian May, host of the podcast Mayday, uploaded a 52-minute video explaining why it won’t be long until the Royal Botanic Gardens becomes “an informal campground”, marking what viewers described as a logical progression for a man who has already spent several years warning that roads, hospitals, schools, energy grids and customer service departments were all approaching terminal failure.

item.title: Podcaster Furious After Followers Start Tagging Him With “Owning Nothing And Being Happy Doesn’t Sound That Bad, Really”
item.description:
LONDON — British podcaster Julian May has reportedly entered a state of ideological emergency after dozens of users began tagging him on X with the phrase, “Owning nothing and being happy doesn’t sound that bad, really.”

May, host of the popular doom podcast Mayday, has spent years warning viewers that global elites intend to strip ordinary people of property, autonomy, privacy, national identity and the spiritual inheritance of Western civilisation before instructing them to “own nothing and be happy”.
